description: In Java/JVM, use the package-local commands (the Maven/Gradle module task (mvn -pl / gradle :module:test) with the right JDK toolchain) rather than a root or generic command.
body: In Java/JVM, the real contract lives in the owning package's config; a root or generic command can use the wrong the JDK version, bytecode target, and JIT/GC configuration or skip checks. Use when building, testing, or analyzing a specific Java/JVM package, especially in a monorepo. Do not assume a top-level command covers the package, and avoid running a generic command that ignores its config. Actions: find the package's own targets, run the Maven/Gradle module task (mvn -pl / gradle :module:test) with the right JDK toolchain and the module's JUnit suite on the affected classpath scoped to it, and compare results against the package's configured gate. Validation signals include the package-scoped command log, its exit code, and a SpotBugs, Error Prone, and Checkstyle reports pass using the package config. Risk boundary: escalate if no package-local command exists or if it depends on environment you cannot safely provision.
